problem: Two fair, six-sided dice are rolled.  What is the probability that the sum of the two numbers showing is less than 11?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe what the problem is asking in your own words?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many possible outcomes are there when rolling two fair, six-sided dice?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you list all the possible outcomes and their corresponding sums?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many of these outcomes have a sum less than 11?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we are finding the probability of the sum being greater than or equal to 11 instead?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How did you determine that there are only 3 pairs of rolls that result in a sum greater than or equal to 11?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How did you calculate the total number of possible pairs of rolls?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why do we use the concept of complementary probabilities in this problem?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How did you calculate the probability of the sum being not less than 11?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we subtract the probability found in step 9 from 1 to get the probability of the sum being less than 11?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final probability that the sum of the numbers showing is less than 11?
classified_questions.label: b
